target.title: fix(plugins): isolate cached tool runtime siblings
target.body:
## Summary

- keeps cached plugin descriptor execution from dropping a healthy tool when the runtime factory also returns a malformed sibling tool
- aligns cached-descriptor execution with discovery-time behavior: malformed tools are isolated, while valid siblings remain usable
- adds a regression covering a cached healthy plugin tool executing successfully after a malformed sibling was skipped during descriptor capture

## Verification

- regression first failed on current `origin/main` with `plugin tool runtime missing (fuzzplugin): mockplugin_status`
- rebased cleanly onto `origin/main` `51dee73a5d3`; current head is `97e877a5706`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts`: 61 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/tools.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/tools.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts`
- `git diff --check` plus touched-file scrub for reported fixture names, private local paths, and install-error output: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings before PR open
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: passed on run `run_96b73113aa80`, lease `cbx_91fe7bafb7db` / `violet-crayfish` (provider=aws, command 3m8s, total 5m25s)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a malformed plugin tool returned from a runtime factory no longer prevents execution of a healthy sibling tool whose descriptor was already cached.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree rebased onto `origin/main` `51dee73a5d3`, plus AWS Crabbox Linux proof for the changed gate on head `97e877a5706`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: created a focused regression that failed before the production change, applied the runtime isolation fix, rebased onto current main, reran the focused plugin test, formatter, lint, diff check, scrub, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: the cached healthy `mockplugin_status` tool resolves and executes even when the same runtime factory returns a malformed `fuzz_move_angles` sibling.

Observed result after fix: cached plugin descriptor execution returns the healthy tool result instead of failing with `plugin tool runtime missing`.

What was not tested: no live external plugin process; this uses the existing plugin registry harness to exercise the cached descriptor runtime path directly.
